speaker: Isabel Schnabel
title: Monetary policy in times of geopolitical fragmentation
tone_comparison: Consistent with her established hawkish-vigilant stance. Schnabel continues to frame geopolitical fragmentation as an argument for — not against — a firm price-stability commitment. Semantic match with her Mar 6 speech: same supply-shock / credibility framing, stronger geopolitical context.
representative_quote: N/A — speech text not available in readable format.

speaker: Luis de Guindos
title: Navigating turbulence: challenges for Europe and the path ahead
tone_comparison: Semantically consistent with his Jan 14 financial stability speech: same data-dependent framing, same emphasis on resilience-under-uncertainty. Slightly softer here — more emphasis on benign inflation baseline and adaptive policy than on tail risks. No new hawkish signal.
representative_quote: A new paradigm has emerged, where the rule of law is challenged by the rule of power. High uncertainty and geoeconomic fragmentation complicate the assessment of the economic outlook.

speaker: Piero Cipollone
title: The digital euro in a fragmenting world: ensuring Europe’s resilience and autonomy in payments
tone_comparison: Fully consistent with his entire 8-week record. All Cipollone speeches cluster semantically around payments infrastructure and European sovereignty — no monetary policy rate content in any of them. Stronger geopolitical framing this week (Baltic energy analogy) but the underlying message is unchanged.
representative_quote: Sovereignty is only as strong as the infrastructure it rests on. And that infrastructure must be resilient — as the Baltics and Europe more broadly learnt the hard way in the energy domain.
